methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
NextSilicon is a Series B-stage AI & Software company that has raised $200M+ to date. Valuation benchmarking draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable entities: 8 private frontier-tech firms (Safe Superintelligence at $32.0B, Physical Intelligence at $5.6B, World Labs at $5.0B, Nominal at $1.0B, Photoroom at $500M, and three others) and 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses total raised ($200M) as a proxy for implied valuation and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $380M. The Peer-Median method establishes the median valuation across the 5-firm same-sector, same-stage sample at $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B.

The two methods produce materially different results, reflecting acute dispersion in frontier-tech Series B valuations. The triangulated range is $323M (low), $2.7B (median), and $5.8B (high). Stage-progression anchors the lower bound; peer medians drive the median and upper bound upward. Key uncertainty remains the appropriate peer weighting given the extreme $500M-to-$32B range and the limited salience of public comparables to pre-profitability AI infrastructure. Resolution of this dispersion requires clarity on NextSilicon's revenue traction, model deployment scale, and capital efficiency relative to outlier peers.
